column2: Penyata Pilihanraya Umum Dewan Rakyat dan Dewan-Dewan Undangan Negeri kecuali Dewan-Dewan Undangan Negeri Kelantan, Sabah dan Sarawak 1978, New Straits Times 7th April 1982, The politics of accommodation: An analysis of the 1978 Malaysian general election (Research notes and discussions papers - Institute of Southeast Asian Studies ; no. 10) Paperback – January 1, 1978 by Ismail Kassim, Malaysian Politics and the 1978 Election edited by Harold Crouch, Lee Kam Hing and Michael Ong. For specific DUN Candidate background details, refer to 1978_Other_Sources_DUN_File

Candidate names follow the Penyata Pilihan Raya Umum Dewan Rakyat dan Dewan-Dewan Undangan Negeri kecuali Dewan-Dewan Undangan Negeri Kelantan, Sabah dan Sarawak 1978. There is a significant number of candidates of which candidate sex category is missing. No data is provided for candidate age. People's Independent Front (Penang) is composed of former MCA members and was originally composed of 12 persons. However, three of the 12 failed to appear for nominations and were eventually reduced to the Gang of 7  (Mah Cheok Tat, Ho Kay Pin, Khoo Soo Kheng, Lim Kah Pin, Khoo Huat Hin, Gan Kah Pheng, Tan Chong Hooi). Ho Kay Pin contested for the Parliamentary seat of Tanjong, while Khoo Soo Kheng contested for the Parliamentary seat of Nibong Tebal.  BN candidates for DUN Kampong Kolam (Penang) (Gerakan was allocated to the seat) and DUN Kuala Kubu Baharu (Selangor) (MCA was allocated to the seat) were disqualified during nominations. The original set of candidates for DUN Kampong Jawa (Selangor) for the 1978 DUN elections was disqualified on nomination day. A separate election was carried out for the DUN Kampong Jawa of which UMNO won the seat. New Straits Times 7th April 1982 claims there were 209 UMNO candidates when the actual number is 208 UMNO candidates. Refer to MALAYSIA_1978_DUN_DISCLAIMERS for errors and contradictions.
